pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in task description and setup_task.sh role parameters.
Explicitly defined in task.json description and verifier docstring, mapping to exhibit design brief.
Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh DATA_FILE variable and exhibit design brief.
Confirmed by reading NetCDF file metadata where variable 'prate' represents precipitation rate.
Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh OUTPUT_DIR variable.
Confirmed by analyzing setup_task.sh instructions and export_result.sh checks.
Python analysis of prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc confirmed the zonal mean precipitation band and absolute max pixel values peak northward in July and August (Months 7 and 8) around latitudes 8.5N to 12.3N.

Python analysis of prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc confirmed the zonal mean precipitation band and absolute max pixel values peak southward in February (Month 2) around latitude 12.3S, alongside broad literature consensus for January/February.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1 climatology data ('prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc') tracking precipitation rates. Python analysis of the NetCDF data corroborates that the Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) reaches its northernmost latitude extremes during July and August, and its southernmost extremes during January and February. The target file paths and specific required output names (itcz_animation.* and lesson_plan.txt) exactly match the export and verification scripts.
